speaker: alice
text: Right, let's kick off with some environmental news that's genuinely got me grinning from ear to ear. Green sea turtles have just been downgraded from endangered to least concern status thanks to absolutely massive conservation efforts. These beautiful creatures were once hunted nearly to extinction for turtle soup - can you believe that?

speaker: charlie
text: That's incredible Jamie, and it shows what we can achieve when we actually put our minds to it. The recovery involved protecting eggs, releasing hatchlings on beaches, and reducing capture in fishing nets. It's taken decades but conservation groups worldwide have pulled off something remarkable here.

speaker: alice
text: And speaking of green wins, Europe's hit a massive milestone with wind and solar power generating more electricity than fossil fuels for the first time ever. Experts are calling it a proper energy tipping point - renewables aren't just an alternative anymore, they're becoming the backbone of the energy system.
